svg.title: How accumulation error grows with FP16 versus FP32 accumulators
svg.desc: Two rows showing k multiply-add steps. Top row uses FP32 accumulator — rounding only happens once at the end. Bottom row uses FP16 accumulator — rounding happens after every add, errors compound across k steps.
svg.text: why FP16 accumulation loses more precision
FP32 accumulator: 1 rounding at the end. FP16 accumulator: k roundings, one per addition step.
For k=1024, FP16 accumulation error is much larger — visible in the max error column of the benchmark.
